sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel movement data documented for this RPG

special_mechanics.name: Real-Time Combat System
special_mechanics.description: Real-time combat with optional pause functionality allowing party orders to be given and executed upon resume. Not a platformer-style movement system.

special_mechanics.name: Party-Based Movement
special_mechanics.description: Player orders characters to engage in movement, dialogue, combat, or actions within game locations. Movement is turn-based/grid-based, not physics-based platforming.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Icewind Dale is a party-based RPG using the Infinity Engine and AD&D 2nd Edition ruleset. It does not feature platformer-style physics. Character movement is menu-driven and tactical rather than real-time physics-based. No precise movement parameters, gravity, jump physics, or sub-pixel systems are documented because the game does not implement these mechanics.
